reviews.positives: One guy on Friday May 30th 2025 Malaysian Time around 8:00 to 9:00 am morning his name may be started with F or maybe his name is Faraz or Fayyaz, when I reached on reception, he welcomed me and I said I have booking from May 30th 2025 to June 03rd, he said Check in time (I have evidence) and I am not lying that 3:00pm which is legitimate, I accepted it, I asked him, can I get it early.. he said “unfortunately no room available” twin bed and all are completely booked and nothing is available, again I accepted in genuine way… I asked we are tired and we need a room and ask your manager and we can pay you difference of extra, he told me he can give us at 1:00 pm but after his offer I asked him to give me on before 1:00 pm talk to your manager about it and he immediately asked him and manager said okay and within space of 5-6 minutes and he looked in computer, room was available.. when I offered to him difference we can pay…. So it is completely unprofessional and unacceptable behavior.. there was no room available and in space of 5-6 minutes, it was available because of money matters? I was ready to pay and room was immediately popped up on computer.. he could have initially told me that pay the difference and we will give you room … he also said “Room wasn’t cleaned” and how it is possible room was cleaned in 5-6 minutes? He said “Good News! Room is available” after I accepted “difference of pay” of MYR 216. It was lying by Hilton Hotel chain hotel? Unprofessional and unacceptable behavior? They can see my conversation in surveillance footage on May 30th, I will address this issue to Hilton Corporate Office.. and corporate should get footage.. He could have declined my room initially by just saying, you came early and room is available but we will be charging you for half day or whatever but this guy lied to me.. I totally understand that I came early and I should have waited.. but I asked him we are tired and we can pay difference..
reviews.negatives: Reception Staff.. Booking.com please publish my review.. don’t hide it or anything, if they will hide it I will prefer to book somewhere else..

reviews.negatives:
About extra bad request. 

 I would like to clarify the timeline regarding the extra bed request for my stay at DoubleTree by Hilton Kuala Lumpur (16–18 February 2026).

• On 15 February 2026 at 23:21 (one day before check-in), I sent a request for an extra bed through Booking.com.
• My check-in was on 16 February, and check-out was on 18 February at 12:00 PM.
• The hotel’s response regarding the extra bed was sent on Thursday(19 FEB) at 14:48, which was after my stay had already ended.

Therefore, the issue was not that I failed to reconfirm. The response itself was sent after my check-out, making it impossible for me to proceed with any confirmation.

reviews.title: Nightmare...
reviews.text:
It was so bad that I have to pen my very first review on this site. 

At about 12.30am, the room suddenly went into total darkness. I thought my wife went to turn off the master switch but realised that it was a power failure. Called up the supposedly 'careline' and was told that "Oh, we have already informed you through a letter about this power outage..." and blabbered some excuses. Anyway, we did not receive the so-called letter and when we checked in that very afternoon, the hotel staff did not alert us of it as well. 

We were told that it will last 20 to 30 mins. In the end, it was a 45-min wait in which we had to open the room door to get some ventilation. We could see people coming out of their rooms and strolling along / sitting at the corridor.

If you think the nightmare is over, wrong!

At about 6am in the morning, the second power failure took place! Same nonchalant response from the 'careline' that there was supposed to be a second of such and it should only last 15 to 20 mins. It was another half an hour of pitch darkness and stuffiness...

Didn't get a proper rest the whole night and couldn't wait to check out the next morning as the holiday experience was already ruined. I have stayed in many hotels at KL previously and this has got to be the worst experience. This incident just reinforced the bad impression i have of Hilton hotels...

Oh btw, they make you pay for the carpark too!

reviews.title: Second Stay Maybe a Mistake
reviews.text: We stayed here just after the hotel opened and this second visit was a real disappointment. The standard of food had dropped considerably. Having paid for the executive floor, we were keen to have a snack and cool drink on arrival. The choice was very average and the hot food was luke warm. We also paid extra for the buffet breakfast and found the standards had slipped from magnificent/outstanding to the point where we wouldn't bother to rebook. Overall, there is better value to be found elsewhere. It's not that easy to get here from the airport either.

reviews.title: Nice rooms but...
reviews.text:
this was certainly not our best experience in staying at a hotel of this class. we experienced problems after problems.

1. when we got into our room, we found that there were still food in the fridge from the previous room guest. 
2. our luggage took 45 mins to reach us and this was with us calling them.
3. when we tried to us the safety box, it was already locked. as we needed to go out urgently, we called the hotel to unlock the safe so that we could use it. they said that their supervisor will be over in a minute. after 15mins, i called again. again, i was told they would be up in a min. after another 30mins. i called and had to speak a little rough. finally, a guy came to fix the safe but he had to suggest that i was the one who locked the safe. i nearly blew my top.

now, here it the shocker. that evening, my wife put on the bathrobe. to her surprised, there was a credit card in the pocket of the robe belonging to the previous guest. they did not change fresh robes for us. we were using soiled bath robes from the previous guest. i wonder if our bedsheets were changed.
when we complained to the manager at the front desk, she certainly did not display a caring attitude. she just mumbled sorry and does not seem to be listening to me.

reviews.title: A two star hotel
reviews.text:
Was really frustrated with the hotel and service provided. Graded as a five star hotel but everything seems to reflect upon it as only a two star hotel. 

1) reservation agent was not professional is replying customers request. Did not try to entertain few of the request but only replied the mail saying that everything is subject to availability. This is a known fact and if I knew this was going to be the answer, I would not bothered to send the request. Was mad not due to the request but due to the manner the agent replied a customer.
2) no wifi in room and only at lobby.
Worst scenario is you will have to wait for 4-5 hours after check in to access the wifi as was told that system needs time to check in. And the final blow is that you can only access the wifi for 30 mins and it will automatically log off. 
So this is not suitable for business traveller at all! What is you are in the midst of a transaction?
3) check out and was not asked to chop any parking ticket. Not aware that this needs to be done, had to go up to the reception again to get it settled.
4) worst of all, was told that I have to pay rm10 for the parking. This was not informed earlier on when I checked in. I did ask but was told that one day free parking given. When the man at the reception told me that I have got to pay, I questioned him and he said that this is not a hotel car park and that guests will have to leave the hotel before 7am if do not want to pay for the car park. What reason given!! Who on earth would leave before 7am the next day when you are on vacation?? 
The money paid is very insignificant but the manner the hotel conducts itself is very out of standard and way below its reputation.

This should be graded a two star hotel instead. Very disappointing experience!
Would never recommend my other business partners and would never return to such hotel! 
Unexpectedly a failure of the management!

reviews.title: “Big Disappointment”
reviews.text:
Stay in KL during Christmas month
Would like to have a special night with my girlfriend since is 12.12.12
I choose double tree thought is a beautiful hotel but it was such a big disappointment.
Check in at 2pm, when in the room found was not the room I wanted so decided to upgrade the room, called the reception 2 times and no one answered, in the end some one called me back and say if I want to change the room I have to wait because rooms are checking out and they need to do cleaning first, ok so I waited after 30min re-called to check on the status they say I have to wait the latest till 5pm!! 
What BS ? You are telling me I'm paying extra and have to wait 3 hours just to clean a 32 sq.m size room? 
In the end I have to go hungry just for the staff to change the room at 4pm
After changing to the other room to my surprise after 2hours of cleaning I still find a bunch of hair on the bathrobe and the bathtub.
I went down to confirm if my room is entitled to the Internet service while i was waiting there was a guest, a Chinese lady complaining about the room and want to change it, I can see the staff answer the guest is so perfunctory saying in Chinese "oh yes ahh" and in a loud tone "we don't have room "
Went to the executive lounge for breakfast, food was not much of a choice,and is so troublesome when I take the food one hand holding the food cover and the other hand holding the plate and there is no more hand to take the food
the air-con was so cold that when I take the food and back to the table is already cold 
Being charge so much and there is no Internet service and free parking and what a bad experience!! I can find much more better hotel in KL which are way more better service with much more facilities. I would not come back here and won't even recommend to a single person to this hotel!!

reviews.title: In need of a serious refresh
reviews.text:
We’ve been loyal guests at DoubleTree Hilton KL, often staying every one to two months, and usually appreciate the warm service and comfortable experience. However, our recent stay was unfortunately quite disappointing due to the condition of the rooms.

We had to change rooms upon check-in because the initial room had an unserviceable WC; the flush wasn’t working, and some power switches were faulty, leaving the room extremely dim. 
After reporting the issues, the staff promptly assisted and moved us to another room on a lower floor, which they mentioned was an “upgraded” room. We appreciate the team’s effort and their friendliness; it’s clear they were doing their best to make our stay more pleasant.

That said, the second room also had its own set of problems. The bathroom sink was clogged and took forever to drain, causing water to splash up easily; which was frustrating when trying to wash up. The ceiling light in the room wasn’t working either, making the whole room feel gloomy and poorly lit. We experienced similar drainage issues in the first room, leading us to believe that the plumbing system is outdated and in serious need of an upgrade.

The poor drainage also caused moisture build-up and a musty smell in the restroom, which made the space feel unpleasant. These issues were reported to the reception, but we understand there’s probably only so much the staff can do given the condition of the infrastructure.
While we commend the hospitality of the team, the overall state of the rooms fell short of expectations—especially from a Hilton-branded property. Until a major renovation takes place, we’ve decided to take a break from staying here, as the hotel is clearly due for a much-needed makeover. This experience felt more in line with a budget hotel than what we expect from Hilton.

reviews.title: Do Not Stay
reviews.text:
To Sum up what is about to be a long review.

This hotel is not a 5 star. It is a 4 star. Firstly, the bell boy did not help my girlfriend with her luggage until she took it out of the taxi herself and was walking away (gf is 54kg! bell hop was huge!) Check in was confusing and we, as well as others, ended up cutting the cue unintentionally. The room was not up to par for a 5 star as it was missing many bathroom amenities that you are used to like: comb, cotton buds, shower cap est. Also, the pillows were hard, the tv did not have many channels and frankly the room was bland. They only have tubs in suites. 

We went out 2x and both times no staff offered the 'free shuttle' to KLCC and no one helped us get a taxi. instead we were pointed to the road where there was a taxi stand. if you know anything about taxis in Malaysia you know how much of a fun experience that was! as the first 3 taxis we hailed the first time refused to run the meter.

The pool was awful! Freezing! The pool gets 0 light as its surrounded by buildings. If you look around the edge you see what seems to be rust. I think it got there from the excessive amount of salt in the pool! It dried out my skin so bad i was itching.

We tried to order breakfast and after we ordered they called back saying they were out of cereal and low fat milk. You are a 5 star hotel! Its a Saturday morning! Upon checkout we were not asked how our stay was, were not smiled at, i just gave in my keys and the receptionist said, "ok thats it" and pointed me away from the desk. While we were leaving to top it all off, the 'welcome drink' area had 0 clean glasses! What kind of a welcome is that?

reviews.title: An experience I’d want to forget..
reviews.text: We are a party of four arriving on separate days with only one simple request to hotel receptionist Najwa: to put the newlyweds room away from the parent’s. We stressed to her that no newlyweds want to stay next door to the parents. No problem, she promised. Unbeknownst to us the newlyweds checked into a room next door, and after a redeye flight went straight to bed. Worst still being the end rooms the parent’s door were at right angle to the newlyweds. What are the odds? When questioned Najwa’s excuses were many: “I did tell someone else about it”?, “at least it’s not a connecting room”, “I can move you to a different room” after we have long settled in and will need to repack all our suitcases and purchases to move. reviews.title: Standard 5 star hotel. Incompetent concierge
reviews.text: This is a standard 5 star city hotel. Nothing amazing but ticks all the boxes. Although I must say that out of the 3 hotels I have stayed in KL, this one has the most inefficient service. It took 2 visits and almost half an hour waiting just to book a taxi for the next morning. First concierge said he would get it done and send me a message early in the evening. When I stepped out of the hotel in the evening, I passed by concierge desk just to check, and the concierge on duty told me nothing was booked. I then asked him to book. The answer was that he is not able to arrange it but he will ask the person in charge of taxi stand to do it for me. Now if a concierge can't even book a taxi I don't know what else they can do. It took 25 minutes for the taxi person to come. Meanwhile the same concierge passed in front of me at least 3 times. When I questioned him he then said he was looking for the person. He wouldn't have bothered to explain if I did not ask. Because of all the waiting I was late for my appointment. I asked the same concierge what was the fastest way to get to Bukit Bintan. The answer was taxi. Knowing that at 7pm in KL it would take at least 45 minutes, I was expecting something else. Again no apology for causing the delay, not willing to help or offer alternatives. I wonder if this was because I refused the hotel limousine service and there was no commission. For this experience I won't go back to stay.

reviews.title: 2 days travel
reviews.text:
Stayed at DoubleTree by Hilton Kuala Lumpur recently. The room itself was generally clean — the towels, bed, and toilet appeared well maintained. However, after using the bathroom, water started accumulating on the toilet floor, which suggests there may be a leakage issue somewhere that needs maintenance attention.

The biggest disappointment was the food quality. The buffet food did not feel fresh, and many dishes seemed to have been left out for too long without being refreshed. For example, the satay sides such as the onions and cucumbers were dry and no longer fresh.

During breakfast, several dishes were overly salty, and the beef, lamb, and fish were very tough and dry, likely due to overcooking. Considering this is a Hilton property, I expected much better food quality and freshness standards.

I hope the hotel can improve the kitchen quality control and food freshness, as the room experience itself was otherwise acceptable.

reviews.title: JUST GO TO ANY OTHER "RESORT" AROUND THE CORNER
reviews.text:
PROS: 
* Best beach in the area (BUT IS PUBLIC, SO ANYONE FROM ANY OTHER HOTEL CAN GO THERE).
* Kind employees, but seems they start working yesterday. 

CONS:
* It is ABSOLUTELY NOT a resort: no alcohol, no life guard at the beach or pools, service sucks, very strict time slots for restaurant, they take away your towels after 6 pm (wtf?).
* Our room walls were not well sealed, so bugs and geckos can get into the rooms easily. 
* Every single thing is from 2 to 5 times more expensive than in other places: food, drinks, basic things like sunblock, plug adapter, etc. 
* Food quality really poor, everything tastes the same. 
* You almost need to beg for things at the restaurant. 

MY ADVICE: book a cheaper and nicer place (better rooms, better food, cheaper things, cheaper sea taxis fees) around the corner.

reviews.text:
Stayed here 1st time.

Already understood Double Tree policy no free wifi provided.  But even in my room, my wifi crawled as the hotel was blocked by other building.  I am checking with my Telco now.

Room was big and comfortable.  My room was 1702, no view as facing the other wing.  Have to draw the curtain all the time.

Enjoyed the rain shower.  But once, the shower room was flooded!!

Only good thing about this hotel - walking distance to Jalan Ampang and many eateries and PETRONAS towers.

I think there are better alternative nearby which is value for money.

reviews.title: Average, I would not recommend

reviews.negatives: While the rooms were satisfactory, the halls emitted a rather unpleasant odor. Overall, the hotel appears to lack proper maintenance, and the rooms lack soundproofing. Even on the 30th floor, noise from traffic and trains was noticeable.

reviews.title: No "wow" factor for a Hilton Group Hotel
reviews.text:
I had expected a better experience from a Hilton Group Hotel. 
But the entire experience is more what I had experienced in other 3-4 star hotel. The check-in guy had no eye contact nor were we offered a welcome drink (but the rasberry tea was really nice!) while we waited for our room to be ready. The welcome cookie (tho warm) was just handed to us as tho we should already know there should be one. 

In our room, the overall decoration was comtemporary but it was nothing to wow about.. the pillows were smelly and hotel view restricted to a toll station nearby. The location is not too far from KLCC nor Bk Bintan be it via the train or taxi. Most staff were more cordial rather than friendly, which does not really add value to the entire hotel experience. Overall, I'd rather pay alittle more for a much better experience at the Hilton near KL Sentral.

To make it right should be one time job and stated clear in the menu to avoid double job. Car park had to pay which feel disappointed and should be bare by hotel. Did not like the taxi touts camping outside the hotel who refused to use the meters. This was in contrast to the official taxi rank directly outside the entrance. Was caught unaware with the security bond. We had to use a credit card which incurred extra, avoidable fees, and only refunded within 14 days.

We waited for another half hour but no news. We got doen to the lobby and talked to ine if manager and she told us to wait for cheking current status. Wait for another 30 min then give us same answer. She brought us to the executive room to wait. So we go there and wait for another 30 min. reviews.text: Not a pleasant start as we were waiting at lobby more than 45 minutes at lobby. This is due to system down which disabled us to check in. While enter the room, found that room is smaller than what we expected. No bath tub at the bathroom. Anyway, the  subway station is 10minutes walking distance. The pedestrian overhead bridge is in progress of the connection between hotel and subway station. Free shuttle also available by hotel 	to major attractions at city such as twin tower, Nikita vibrant and China town. Location wise, the hotel is scoring high, however the services need to be further improved.

reviews.title: Generally tried their best
reviews.text:
had a great room on the 34th floor but was shocked & disappointed to find a tiny baby cockroach crawling on my pillow before bed. requested for a room change and instead of getting upgraded, got downgraded to a smaller room on the 16th floor with a towel rack that was not fixed in properly & kept dropping on one side. i guess it was booked out for f1 weekend but still...

when our room wasn't made after lunch i called to request for it. the careline receptionist made sure to call and check ten mins later that the request had been carried out so that was a plus...could've used some of that attention to detail from the check-in desk.
reviews.positives: Pillow Menu is a great touch and so were the Crabtree & Evelyn toiletries

reviews.title: Convenient Location Rooms not in well maintained
reviews.text:
You know full well that the lobby and accessible surrounding areas will reflect t6he Hilton name in all its glory, and expect the individual rooms to be, well, Hilton standard. They were not. We stayed 3 days in all, and the room was clean ... ish, and the bathroom was more ish than clean. There was a poor repair in the shower, with mould ingrained, indicating this was long term, and the room servicing was just a bit off. 
The room was however comfortable and there were no problems sleeping. The reception staff were generally enthusiastic in their efforts, but with a wide range of ability level.
Overall a pleasant stay, but not up to expected standards.

reviews.title: 4 starts hotel
reviews.text:
Negative: This is a hotel that used to be five stars but is now outdated and poorly maintained. The carpets in the hallways are in terrible condition — they have a very strong, unpleasant odor and are visibly dirty. The furniture and rooms are in need of modernization. POSITIVE: It has a gym, which is not fully equipped but still usable for training. It's located on the first floor of a shopping mall, which makes many things convenient (there’s a supermarket, restaurants, and cafés).

If you're able to walk and use stairs, the location is good. However, for elderly guests, private transportation may be necessary.

reviews.title: Cleanliness issue
reviews.text: I had a disappointing experience at this hotel due to cleanliness and service issues. First, our check-in was delayed with no explanation, leaving us waiting for an hour (2nd room checked in 3.45pm) after a long day of travel. When we finally got to our room, we immediately noticed a strong, unpleasant smell coming from the bathroom. Despite bringing it up to the staff, the issue persisted throughout our stay, with the odor making the room uncomfortable. I expect a basic level of cleanliness and service from any hotel, but unfortunately, this stay fell short. I would consider recommend this hotel based on our experience.

reviews.title: The room is not ready after 3 pm
reviews.text: I arrived at 3:48 pm, but I was informed that the room was not ready. I was given the room key at 4:30 pm. Check-in time should be at 3 pm and the room should be ready before 3 pm. This hotel doesn't deserve five stars!  Bad service.

reviews.title: Great location but not built to handle capacity
reviews.text:
The location is really good. It is attached to a mall and very accessible by public transportation. 
The negative is that the customer has to stand in queue for everything. There was a queue to check-in. There was a very long queue every morning to get into the breakfast restaurant. 15-20 mins was the average wait time which is problematic if you have activities planned after breakfast. I would not expect this from a 5 star hotel.

reviews.title: Customer service with zero empathy or ownership to issues
reviews.text: Went out and return on the night to find my card not working to open the door. Called careline, of which they said wait for the key. After 20 minutes of waiting, I called again, and another person answered saying there’s no record of my request earlier, again told me to wait and it took 10 minutes. At the end, I brought my senior parent who was having stomachache to the lobby at 11 midnight to get the key card myself. When I made the complaint to the front desk, he wasn’t even listening to what I’m saying or empathised for the half an hour wait outside the room floor corridor. He just advised as though I brought the power keycard when actually I didn’t. Food was good. But seriously, this hotel doesn’t give the 5-star feel at all

reviews.title: More Improvement Needed!
reviews.text:
1. Check-in process took too long and exceeded 3pm check-in time. Would recommend to activate mobile web check-in since the hotel requires each and everyone information who stays in the hotel (took all our ICs). 
2. The location of the hotel entrance is not convenient since it shares same entrance with Intermark and its not located on the same lobby. No concierge at the drop off at the entrance, hence we had to carry our luggage on our own from the parking to the lobby.
3. The food I would give 5/10. Same menu on both days that I stayed. And it was tasteless especially the sambal nasi lemak and teh tarik is too much milk. Should improve on this as these 2 are the national dish & beverage.

reviews.title: ‘Privacy at its best’ NOT!
reviews.text: My stay was overall pleasant but I’d like to thank the staff who deserves the credits and for some to improve in the future.  It was a busy weekend yet thank you to Odelia for handling my complaint professionally. To housekeeping team,  please triple check before getting into a guest’s room at 8pm which clearly I had extended my stay beforehand. It was a scary moment when someone trying to get into your room with force when DND sign on and door was locked. At that moment, I thought it was an intruder. No idea why a call can’t be made when it’s the simplest thing to do/check.

reviews.title: Good Hotel

Good 1 night stay. 
The fridge in the room wasn't working, but have it settled fast when called.
However, a few things can be improved/fixed:
- When during check in, to have a better way to make it smoother especially during holidays. We have to queue up for an hour to check in.
-To check the bidet in the bathrooms - the bidet in my room was leaking.
-Ensure that all lifts are available and work properly during peak seasons. You have the entire hotel trying to check out at the same time. The lifts have to close its doors 10 times before they can go down the next floor.

reviews.title: No SOPs for Covid-19 and 4 hours to check in!
reviews.text:
The short stay at Doubletree for our birthdays was mainly to avoid crowds due to the current pandemic. We were told check in starts at 3pm and to our shock, the queue was ridiculously long with NO social distancing measures applied by DoubleTree. There were at least 50+ people at the queue.

Fearing the spread of Covid-19, we decided to have a drink at a cafe nearby and check on the queue every hour. However, the queue was either the same or worse. After waiting from 3pm to 6.15pm, the queue was equally long. We had no choice but to start queuing - with a possible risk of infection.

We queued for almost an hour before arriving at the check in counter. When asked about whether the hours of stay will be extended due to the long ridiculous wait, the answer from front desk was "This is not in our control" and were given only 2 hours late check out; with no apologies.

I'm disappointed with the risk DoubleTree has put so many people through and demand a proper explanation or apology.

reviews.title: Great place  but Staff appalling this time around
reviews.text:
This is the second time i stayed at this hotel. The first time the stay was excellent and that is why i returned. This time around the stay was great and generally all the staff was great as usual but at check out day the on duty European customer service manager was just appalling. 
At the time of check in i had confirmed a late check out till a particular time which was late evening and upgraded to the special access level as i always do. On the day of check out this girl come to my room and start to bang the door for us to check out. After explaining, she still argues that i need to check out. After explaining her that she needs to see her system for notes put in by the staff that checked me in, she will find the information, she very reluctantly goes away and within 5 mins start to call the room every 5 mins. all this happening at the time i was trying to get some rest before my long international Flight. After a long going back and forth and after an escalation to her supervisor the situation was resolved and that too the supervisor trying to tell me that he will do something special for me the next time i come to stay after ruining my last day for which i paid top dollars to the hotel to stay longer, will i stay again at this hotel, possibly not as there are other option in the city of comparable luxury and price point . The thing that got to me was the rudeness of the staff initially.
In fairness, the rest of the stay has always been great at this hotel. But what i fail to understand is how can a staff working at such a five star international chain hotel get away after being so rude one of the patrons. Well with your stay i hope you will not have a similar experience.
reviews.positives: Cleanliness, New, Views from the room

reviews.title: Great rooms marred by poor dining
reviews.text: Being surrounded by great dining establishments saved the day. Room service food was appalling as seafood served was rubbery. Service staff at the Cellar Door/Axis Lounge seemed to be lost when it came to ordering drinks and there was quite a wait even for a simple bill. The Ploughman's Lunch served with charcuterie option was rather odd and caused stomach discomfort after, the kitchen also forgot to serve cheese with it! The lifts however do need to be seen to as there is a long wait for the lift and some doors even close on you before you can get in. Friendly valet staff encourage me to head back there again as nothing beats a warm smile when you arrive. Recommended for guests who do not plan to dine at the hotel.
reviews.positives: Comfortable beds, generous bathroom, great valet staff

However its more suitable for business travelling rather than family vacation...
